intro: Today we are ranking Extra Virgin Olive Oil as a fat, per 100g, so you can see where it really lands.

sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 100g. this whole verdict comes down to fat quality. this is where things can go wrong fast.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 100g. this whole verdict comes down to fat quality. this is where things can go wrong fast.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 0g. everything else is kind of whatever. for a fat, this starts with the fatty acid profile and only then moves to use case.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 0g. everything else is kind of whatever. for a fat, this starts with the fatty acid profile and only then moves to use case.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 0g. everything else here is pretty flat. for oils and fats, you are basically judging the chemistry with a practical filter.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 0g. everything else here is pretty flat. for oils and fats, you are basically judging the chemistry with a practical filter.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 93% DV, vitamin k at 50% DV stand out most.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 93% DV, vitamin k at 50% DV stand out most.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 3% DV, magnesium at 0% DV stand out most. everything else is fairly underwhelming.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 3% DV, magnesium at 0% DV stand out most. everything else is fairly underwhelming.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: biggest pros here. excellent oils-and-fats category fit. contains useful polyphenols. very simple ingredient identity.

displayItems.title: excellent oils-and-fats category fit
displayItems.explanation: This is the cleanest flagship example of a strong fat-profile food in the category.

displayItems.title: contains useful polyphenols
displayItems.explanation: Adds extra value beyond the core fat chemistry alone.

displayItems.title: very simple ingredient identity
displayItems.explanation: A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

sections.subtitleText: biggest pros here. excellent oils-and-fats category fit. contains useful polyphenols. very simple ingredient identity.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: biggest cons here. extremely energy dense. quality varies a lot. saturated fat is not trivial.

displayItems.title: extremely energy dense
displayItems.explanation: Still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast.

displayItems.title: quality varies a lot
displayItems.explanation: Source and authenticity matter in practice.

displayItems.title: saturated fat is not trivial
displayItems.explanation: Still much cleaner than weaker fats, but not negligible.

sections.subtitleText: biggest cons here. extremely energy dense. quality varies a lot. saturated fat is not trivial.

closing.summary: Overall, Extra Virgin Olive Oil lands in S tier.
